meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't collaborate (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Fix Collaboration Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with silos. Learn how to implement a unified analytics setup to solve collaboration bottlenecks using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To solve collaboration silos, decouple your analytics layer from the codebase using a centralized event-bus architecture. By implementing a standardized schema managed via Antigravity and automated through Claude Code, you create a 'single source of truth' that allows non-technical stakeholders to audit data without touching your production logic.
content.intro:
The midnight ping from a client: 'Why is the dashboard showing zero conversions?' You scramble, digging through fragmented logs, realizing your analytics implementation is a black box that only you can decipher. Collaboration dies when data is trapped in silos, leaving you as the permanent bottleneck for every minor business insight.

We are moving beyond the 'hardcoded events' era. By treating your analytics as infrastructure rather than a UI afterthought, you create a transparent, self-documenting pipeline. This shift doesn't just save your sanity; it turns you into a strategic partner who delivers actionable data, not just lines of code.

steps.title: Define the Universal Event Schema
steps.description: Standardize your events to prevent data drift. If your tracking isn't typed, it isn't reliable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a unified analytics event schema that supports user identity, event_name, metadata, and timestamp fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate analytics/schema.ts based on the provided event interface'

steps.title: Decouple Tracking Logic
steps.description: Create an abstraction layer so your frontend logic remains clean and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build an AnalyticsProvider class that implements a singleton pattern to handle event dispatching to multiple destinations (e.g., Mixpanel, Segment, Console).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement src/analytics/provider.ts using the singleton pattern'

steps.title: Automate Event Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to wrap your critical user flows with tracking hooks without manual boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify critical user interaction points in the project and suggest a decorator pattern or hook to inject analytics events automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Wrap all API calls in src/services with trackEvent calls'

steps.title: Enable Collaborative Audit Logs
steps.description: Expose analytics events to a dashboard where clients can verify data flow themselves.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a local JSON-based audit log middleware that writes all dispatched events to a /logs/analytics.json file for easy inspection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add analytics middleware to capture all outgoing events to a local file'

steps.title: CI/CD Validation
steps.description: Ensure no breaking changes reach production by validating the schema during builds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Zod validation script that runs during build time to verify all event calls match the established schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add build step to validate analytics events against schema.ts'

pro_tips: Always log events to a local file in development; it allows clients to verify data before you deploy to production.
Use Zod for runtime validation of event payloads to ensure your analytics dashboard never receives malformed data.
Tag your analytics events with 'version numbers' in the metadata to track which version of your app generated the data.
Avoid tracking sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) by implementing a whitelist-only filter in your AnalyticsProvider.
Map your analytics events to business KPIs directly in the code comments so future developers know exactly why a specific event exists.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics layer is unified, deploy your audit dashboard to a private Vercel instance and share the read-only link with your client to kill the 'is this working?' email loop forever.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in isolation and start shipping systems that your clients can actually interact with. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
